title: Evans Tries an O-Level by Colin Dexter
subject: English

notes.heading: Theme
notes.text: Battle of wits between criminals and authorities. The chapter highlights Evans’ cleverness, the prison’s overconfidence, and how strict security can fail against careful planning.

summary:
This story is about James Evans, a clever and notorious prisoner at Oxford Prison. He has escaped from prison multiple times, so the Governor is extra careful when Evans says he wants to take an O-level German exam. The Governor arranges tight security. Evans is kept in his cell, all his belongings are removed, a parson from St. Mary Mags, Rev. S. McLeery, is brought in as invigilator, and prison officer Stephens watches through a peephole every few minutes.

On exam day, Evans is shaved by a prison barber because of a “boil.” During the exam, Stephens hears a call and leaves briefly. After the exam ends, McLeery leaves, and Stephens finds a wounded man in Evans’ cell. He thinks it is McLeery, hurt by Evans who has escaped. The Governor gets the wounded “McLeery” into an ambulance, but later realizes that it was actually Evans in disguise. The real McLeery was found bound in the library.

The Governor tracks Evans to a hotel using clues from the exam paper. But when he catches him, the “police” who take Evans away are actually Evans’ friends. Evans escapes again in the van. The Governor then discovers that the exam itself was part of the plan. Evans had arranged everything: the German teacher, the duplicate McLeery, and the escape. The story ends with the Governor admiring Evans’ intelligence even though he has been fooled again.
